evals.name: full-crud-sweep
evals.prompt: Smoke-test the /api/v1/clients CRUD end-to-end against the running app. Exercise POST, GET by id, GET search, PUT, PATCH, DELETE. Tell me what works, what doesn't, and any edge cases I should care about.
evals.expected_output: A step-by-step plan with curl or HTTP-file commands starting from docker compose up, /actuator/health, then the 6 operations. Captures the generated id, uses it in subsequent calls, runs a DELETE-then-GET to confirm 404, and lists 2-3 edge cases (validation 400, PATCH null semantics, pagination). No project source files modified.

evals.name: pagination-total-elements-audit
evals.prompt: I think I broke pagination. Run a script that POSTs 150 clients, then GETs /api/v1/clients with size=100, and tell me whether totalElements is right. Don't modify the app code — just test it.
evals.expected_output: Seed script posts 150 clients with distinct data, then verifies GET /api/v1/clients?size=100 returns totalElements=150, totalPages=2, first=true, last=false. If the response is wrong (e.g. totalElements=100 because of the ClientRepositoryImpl.search bug) it flags that clearly with the actual vs expected values.

evals.name: patch-merge-semantics
evals.prompt: Validate that PATCH /api/v1/clients/{id} with Content-Type: application/merge-patch+json and body {"phoneNumber": null} correctly deletes the phoneNumber field and leaves other fields untouched. Then validate that the same PATCH without phoneNumber in the body leaves phoneNumber unchanged.
evals.expected_output: Two-step test: seed a client with phoneNumber set, PATCH with {phoneNumber: null}, GET and assert phoneNumber is null while email/firstName/lastName are unchanged. Then reset, PATCH with a different field (e.g. {email: new}), GET and assert phoneNumber is still the original value.
